Today, it is located behind the main hall of {{ill|Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja|至宝山金光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with parts of the mound modified for cemetery use. Archaeological excavations by the Iwaki City Board of Education from [[:en:2004|2004]] to [[:en:2007|2007]] revealed key features of the tumulus: * The mound is built in three tiers in the circular section, while the exact structure of the front section remains uncertain. * Total length: 112 meters (third largest in Fukushima, fourth in Tōhoku). * Outer surface covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]], though no haniwa have been found. * Burial facility remains unexcavated; excavated artifacts include shards of double-lipped earthenware jars. The tomb's construction is associated with the early expansion of large-scale kofun in southern Tōhoku. Comparisons have been drawn with [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (114 meters) and [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (110 meters), indicating links to Yamato rulers and regional governance. == Historical Significance == The site has been historically documented in records related to Konkoji Temple and has been surveyed multiple times: * Edo period: Mentioned in temple records of Konkoji. * 1940 (Shōwa 15): First introduced in academic literature. * 1979 (Shōwa 54): Surveyed for size and structure. * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Designated a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. * 2004–2007: Excavations confirmed mound structure and burial context. == Mound Dimensions == According to the 2004–2007 excavation survey: * Total length: approximately 112 meters. * Circular rear section: ** Diameter: approximately 60 meters ** Height: approximately 10 meters * Rectangular front section: ** Length: approximately 54 meters ** Height: approximately 6 meters Previous estimates of 118 meters were revised after precise measurements of the foundation stones. Further investigations may reveal additional extensions. A structure near the mound base has been identified as a possible mourning site for funerary rituals. == Cultural Properties == === Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site === * Tamayama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 1980. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Iwaki City Board of Education (2009). {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|37.114994|N|140.943828|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48753501]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tamayama Kofun''' (玉山古墳, also known as '''Tamayama No.1 Kofun''' or '''Konkoji Kofun''') is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Tamayama Kofun Group|ja|玉山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been designated as a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. This kofun Ranks as the third largest in Fukushima Prefecture and the fourth largest in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]]. It is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == Tamayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tomb ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) situated on a flat terrace along the northern bank of the Niida River in eastern Fukushima. Today, it is located behind the main hall of {{ill|Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja|至宝山金光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with parts of the mound modified for cemetery use. Archaeological excavations by the Iwaki City Board of Education from [[:en:2004|2004]] to [[:en:2007|2007]] revealed key features of the tumulus: * The mound is built in three tiers in the circular section, while the exact structure of the front section remains uncertain. * Total length: 112 meters (third largest in Fukushima, fourth in Tōhoku). * Outer surface covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]], though no haniwa have been found. * Burial facility remains unexcavated; excavated artifacts include shards of double-lipped earthenware jars. The tomb's construction is associated with the early expansion of large-scale kofun in southern Tōhoku. Comparisons have been drawn with [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (114 meters) and [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (110 meters), indicating links to Yamato rulers and regional governance. == Historical Significance == The site has been historically documented in records related to Konkoji Temple and has been surveyed multiple times: * Edo period: Mentioned in temple records of Konkoji. * 1940 (Shōwa 15): First introduced in academic literature. * 1979 (Shōwa 54): Surveyed for size and structure. * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Designated a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. * 2004–2007: Excavations confirmed mound structure and burial context. == Mound Dimensions == According to the 2004–2007 excavation survey: * Total length: approximately 112 meters. * Circular rear section: ** Diameter: approximately 60 meters ** Height: approximately 10 meters * Rectangular front section: ** Length: approximately 54 meters ** Height: approximately 6 meters Previous estimates of 118 meters were revised after precise measurements of the foundation stones. Further investigations may reveal additional extensions. A structure near the mound base has been identified as a possible mourning site for funerary rituals. == Cultural Properties == === Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site === * Tamayama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 1980. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Iwaki City Board of Education (2009). ''Tamayama Kofun: Excavation Report.'' * Kawazaki, Juntoku (1989). ''Japanese Kofun Dictionary.'' Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing. * Heibonsha (1993). ''History and Topography of Japan: Fukushima Prefecture.'' == Related Sites == * {{ill|Tsukamae Kofun|ja|塚前古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamae Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki City) - A later kofun, estimated at 95-120 meters. * {{ill|Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585422}} - Regional rulers potentially associated with Tamayama Kofun. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhol ... 5 KB (646 words) - 05:31, 20 January 2025 List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima) | align="center"|[[Tamayama Kofun]]

It is part of the {{ill|Tamayama Kofun Group|ja|玉山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun Group|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been designated as a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. This kofun Ranks as the third largest in Fukushima Prefecture and the fourth largest in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]]. It is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == Tamayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tomb ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) situated on a flat terrace along the northern bank of the Niida River in eastern Fukushima. Today, it is located behind the main hall of {{ill|Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja|至宝山金光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with parts of the mound modified for cemetery use. Archaeological excavations by the Iwaki City Board of Education from [[:en:2004|2004]] to [[:en:2007|2007]] revealed key features of the tumulus: * The mound is built in three tiers in the circular section, while the exact structure of the front section remains uncertain. * Total length: 112 meters (third largest in Fukushima, fourth in Tōhoku). * Outer surface covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]], though no haniwa have been found. * Burial facility remains unexcavated; excavated artifacts include shards of double-lipped earthenware jars. The tomb's construction is associated with the early expansion of large-scale kofun in southern Tōhoku. Comparisons have been drawn with [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (114 meters) and [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (110 meters), indicating links to Yamato rulers and regional governance. == Historical Significance == The site has been historically documented in records related to Konkoji Temple and has been surveyed multiple times: * Edo period: Mentioned in temple records of Konkoji. * 1940 (Shōwa 15): First introduced in academic literature. * 1979 (Shōwa 54): Surveyed for size and structure. * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Designated a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. * 2004–2007: Excavations confirmed mound structure and burial context. == Mound Dimensions == According to the 2004–2007 excavation survey: * Total length: approximately 112 meters. * Circular rear section: ** Diameter: approximately 60 meters ** Height: approximately 10 meters * Rectangular front section: ** Length: approximately 54 meters ** Height: approximately 6 meters Previous estimates of 118 meters were revised after precise measurements of the foundation stones. Further investigations may reveal additional extensions. A structure near the mound base has been identified as a possible mourning site for funerary rituals. == Cultural Properties == === Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site === * Tamayama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 1980. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Iwaki City Board of Education (2009). ''Tamayama Kofun: Excavation Report.'' * Kawazaki, Juntoku (1989). ''Japanese Kofun Dictionary.'' Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing. * Heibonsha (1993). ''History and Topography of Japan: Fukushima Prefecture.'' == Related Sites == * {{ill|Tsukamae Kofun|ja|塚前古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamae Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki City) - A later kofun, estimated at 95-120 meters. * {{ill|Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585422}} - Regional rulers potentially associated with Tamayama Kofun. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhol ... 5 KB (646 words) - 05:31, 20 January 2025 List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima) | align="center"|[[Tamayama Kofun]]

[[ja:玉山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|37.114994|N|140.943828|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q48753501]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tamayama Kofun''' (玉山古墳, also known as '''Tamayama No.1 Kofun''' or '''Konkoji Kofun''') is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Tamayama Kofun Group|ja|玉山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been designated as a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. This kofun Ranks as the third largest in Fukushima Prefecture and the fourth largest in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]]. It is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == Tamayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tomb ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) situated on a flat terrace along the northern bank of the Niida River in eastern Fukushima. Today, it is located behind the main hall of {{ill|Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja|至宝山金光寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with parts of the mound modified for cemetery use. Archaeological excavations by the Iwaki City Board of Education from [[:en:2004|2004]] to [[:en:2007|2007]] revealed key features of the tumulus: * The mound is built in three tiers in the circular section, while the exact structure of the front section remains uncertain. * Total length: 112 meters (third largest in Fukushima, fourth in Tōhoku). * Outer surface covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]], though no haniwa have been found. * Burial facility remains unexcavated; excavated artifacts include shards of double-lipped earthenware jars. The tomb's construction is associated with the early expansion of large-scale kofun in southern Tōhoku. Comparisons have been drawn with [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (114 meters) and [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (110 meters), indicating links to Yamato rulers and regional governance. == Historical Significance == The site has been historically documented in records related to Konkoji Temple and has been surveyed multiple times: * Edo period: Mentioned in temple records of Konkoji. * 1940 (Shōwa 15): First introduced in academic literature. * 1979 (Shōwa 54): Surveyed for size and structure. * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Designated a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. * 2004–2007: Excavations confirmed mound structure and burial context. == Mound Dimensions == According to the 2004–2007 excavation survey: * Total length: approximately 112 meters. * Circular rear section: ** Diameter: approximately 60 meters ** Height: approximately 10 meters * Rectangular front section: ** Length: approximately 54 meters ** Height: approximately 6 meters Previous estimates of 118 meters were revised after precise measurements of the foundation stones. Further investigations may reveal additional extensions. A structure near the mound base has been identified as a possible mourning site for funerary rituals. == Cultural Properties == === Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site === * Tamayama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 1980. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Iwaki City Board of Education (2009). ''Tamayama Kofun: Excavation Report.'' * Kawazaki, Juntoku (1989). ''Japanese Kofun Dictionary.'' Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing. * Heibonsha (1993). ''History and Topography of Japan: Fukushima Prefecture.'' == Related Sites == * {{ill|Tsukamae Kofun|ja|塚前古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamae Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki City) - A later kofun, estimated at 95-120 meters. * {{ill|Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585422}} - Regional rulers potentially associated with Tamayama Kofun. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhol ... 5 KB (646 words) - 05:31, 20 January 2025 List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima) | align="center"|[[Tamayama Kofun]]

[[ja:玉山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|37.114994|N|140.943828|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q48753501]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tamayama Kofun''' (玉山古墳, also known as '''Tamayama No.1 Kofun''' or '''Konkoji Kofun''') is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Tamayama Kofun Group|ja|玉山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been designated as a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. This kofun Ranks as the third largest in Fukushima Prefecture and the fourth largest in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]]. It is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == Tamayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tomb ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) situated on a flat terrace along the northern bank of the Niida River in eastern Fukushima. Today, it is located behind the main hall of {{ill|Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja|至宝山金光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with parts of the mound modified for cemetery use. Archaeological excavations by the Iwaki City Board of Education from [[:en:2004|2004]] to [[:en:2007|2007]] revealed key features of the tumulus: * The mound is built in three tiers in the circular section, while the exact structure of the front section remains uncertain. * Total length: 112 meters (third largest in Fukushima, fourth in Tōhoku). * Outer surface covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]], though no haniwa have been found. * Burial facility remains unexcavated; excavated artifacts include shards of double-lipped earthenware jars. The tomb's construction is associated with the early expansion of large-scale kofun in southern Tōhoku. Comparisons have been drawn with [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (114 meters) and [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (110 meters), indicating links to Yamato rulers and regional governance. == Historical Significance == The site has been historically documented in records related to Konkoji Temple and has been surveyed multiple times: * Edo period: Mentioned in temple records of Konkoji. * 1940 (Shōwa 15): First introduced in academic literature. * 1979 (Shōwa 54): Surveyed for size and structure. * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Designated a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. * 2004–2007: Excavations confirmed mound structure and burial context. == Mound Dimensions == According to the 2004–2007 excavation survey: * Total length: approximately 112 meters. * Circular rear section: ** Diameter: approximately 60 meters ** Height: approximately 10 meters * Rectangular front section: ** Length: approximately 54 meters ** Height: approximately 6 meters Previous estimates of 118 meters were revised after precise measurements of the foundation stones. Further investigations may reveal additional extensions. A structure near the mound base has been identified as a possible mourning site for funerary rituals. == Cultural Properties == === Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site === * Tamayama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 1980. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Iwaki City Board of Education (2009). ''Tamayama Kofun: Excavation Report.'' * Kawazaki, Juntoku (1989). ''Japanese Kofun Dictionary.'' Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing. * Heibonsha (1993). ''History and Topography of Japan: Fukushima Prefecture.'' == Related Sites == * {{ill|Tsukamae Kofun|ja|塚前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamae Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki City) - A later kofun, estimated at 95-120 meters. * {{ill|Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585422}} - Regional rulers potentially associated with Tamayama Kofun. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhol ... 5 KB (646 words) - 05:31, 20 January 2025 List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima) | align="center"|[[Tamayama Kofun]]

A structure near the mound base has been identified as a possible mourning site for funerary rituals. == Cultural Properties == === Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site === * Tamayama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 1980. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Iwaki City Board of Education (2009). ''Tamayama Kofun: Excavation Report.'' * Kawazaki, Juntoku (1989). ''Japanese Kofun Dictionary.'' Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing. * Heibonsha (1993). [[ja:玉山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|37.114994|N|140.943828|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q48753501]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tamayama Kofun''' (玉山古墳, also known as '''Tamayama No.1 Kofun''' or '''Konkoji Kofun''') is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Tamayama Kofun Group|ja|玉山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been designated as a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. This kofun Ranks as the third largest in Fukushima Prefecture and the fourth largest in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]]. It is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == Tamayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tomb ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) situated on a flat terrace along the northern bank of the Niida River in eastern Fukushima. Today, it is located behind the main hall of {{ill|Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja|至宝山金光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with parts of the mound modified for cemetery use. Archaeological excavations by the Iwaki City Board of Education from [[:en:2004|2004]] to [[:en:2007|2007]] revealed key features of the tumulus: * The mound is built in three tiers in the circular section, while the exact structure of the front section remains uncertain. * Total length: 112 meters (third largest in Fukushima, fourth in Tōhoku). * Outer surface covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]], though no haniwa have been found. * Burial facility remains unexcavated; excavated artifacts include shards of double-lipped earthenware jars. The tomb's construction is associated with the early expansion of large-scale kofun in southern Tōhoku. Comparisons have been drawn with [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (114 meters) and [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (110 meters), indicating links to Yamato rulers and regional governance. == Historical Significance == The site has been historically documented in records related to Konkoji Temple and has been surveyed multiple times: * Edo period: Mentioned in temple records of Konkoji. * 1940 (Shōwa 15): First introduced in academic literature. * 1979 (Shōwa 54): Surveyed for size and structure. * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Designated a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. * 2004–2007: Excavations confirmed mound structure and burial context. == Mound Dimensions == According to the 2004–2007 excavation survey: * Total length: approximately 112 meters. * Circular rear section: ** Diameter: approximately 60 meters ** Height: approximately 10 meters * Rectangular front section: ** Length: approximately 54 meters ** Height: approximately 6 meters Previous estimates of 118 meters were revised after precise measurements of the foundation stones. Further investigations may reveal additional extensions. A structure near the mound base has been identified as a possible mourning site for funerary rituals. == Cultural Properties == === Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site === * Tamayama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 1980. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Iwaki City Board of Education (2009). ''Tamayama Kofun: Excavation Report.'' * Kawazaki, Juntoku (1989). ''Japanese Kofun Dictionary.'' Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing. * Heibonsha (1993). ''History and Topography of Japan: Fukushima Prefecture.'' == Related Sites == * {{ill|Tsukamae Kofun|ja|塚前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamae Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki City) - A later kofun, estimated at 95-120 meters. * {{ill|Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585422}} - Regional rulers potentially associated with Tamayama Kofun. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhol ... 5 KB (646 words) - 05:31, 20 January 2025 List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima) | align="center"|[[Tamayama Kofun]]

[[ja:玉山古墳]] [[ja:玉山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|37.114994|N|140.943828|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[da:d:Q48753501]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tamayama Kofun''' (玉山古墳, also known as '''Tamayama No.1 Kofun''' or '''Konkoji Kofun''') is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Tamayama Kofun Group|ja|玉山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been designated as a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. This kofun Ranks as the third largest in Fukushima Prefecture and the fourth largest in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]]. It is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == Tamayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tomb ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) situated on a flat terrace along the northern bank of the Niida River in eastern Fukushima. Today, it is located behind the main hall of {{ill|Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja|至宝山金光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with parts of the mound modified for cemetery use. Archaeological excavations by the Iwaki City Board of Education from [[:en:2004|2004]] to [[:en:2007|2007]] revealed key features of the tumulus: * The mound is built in three tiers in the circular section, while the exact structure of the front section remains uncertain. * Total length: 112 meters (third largest in Fukushima, fourth in Tōhoku). * Outer surface covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]], though no haniwa have been found. * Burial facility remains unexcavated; excavated artifacts include shards of double-lipped earthenware jars. The tomb's construction is associated with the early expansion of large-scale kofun in southern Tōhoku. Comparisons have been drawn with [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (114 meters) and [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (110 meters), indicating links to Yamato rulers and regional governance. == Historical Significance == The site has been historically documented in records related to Konkoji Temple and has been surveyed multiple times: * Edo period: Mentioned in temple records of Konkoji. * 1940 (Shōwa 15): First introduced in academic literature. * 1979 (Shōwa 54): Surveyed for size and structure. * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Designated a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. * 2004–2007: Excavations confirmed mound structure and burial context. == Mound Dimensions == According to the 2004–2007 excavation survey: * Total length: approximately 112 meters. * Circular rear section: ** Diameter: approximately 60 meters ** Height: approximately 10 meters * Rectangular front section: ** Length: approximately 54 meters ** Height: approximately 6 meters Previous estimates of 118 meters were revised after precise measurements of the foundation stones. Further investigations may reveal additional extensions. A structure near the mound base has been identified as a possible mourning site for funerary rituals. == Cultural Properties == === Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site === * Tamayama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 1980. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Iwaki City Board of Education (2009). ''Tamayama Kofun: Excavation Report.'' * Kawazaki, Juntoku (1989). ''Japanese Kofun Dictionary.'' Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing. * Heibonsha (1993). ''History and Topography of Japan: Fukushima Prefecture.'' == Related Sites == * {{ill|Tsukamae Kofun|ja|塚前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamae Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki City) - A later kofun, estimated at 95-120 meters. * {{ill|Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585422}} - Regional rulers potentially associated with Tamayama Kofun. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhol ... 5 KB (646 words) - 05:31, 20 January 2025 List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima) | align="center"|[[Tamayama Kofun]]

[[ja:玉山古墳]] [[ja:玉山古墳]] [[ja:玉山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|37.114994|N|140.943828|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48753501]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:49, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tamayama Kofun''' (玉山古墳, also known as '''Tamayama No.1 Kofun''' or '''Konkoji Kofun''') is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iwaki, Fukushima|Iwaki, Fukushima]], [[:en:Fukushima Prefecture|Fukushima Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Tamayama Kofun Group|ja|玉山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been designated as a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. This kofun Ranks as the third largest in Fukushima Prefecture and the fourth largest in the [[:en:Tōhoku region|Tōhoku region]]. It is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]]. == Overview == Tamayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tomb ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) situated on a flat terrace along the northern bank of the Niida River in eastern Fukushima. Today, it is located behind the main hall of {{ill|Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja|至宝山金光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with parts of the mound modified for cemetery use. Archaeological excavations by the Iwaki City Board of Education from [[:en:2004|2004]] to [[:en:2007|2007]] revealed key features of the tumulus: * The mound is built in three tiers in the circular section, while the exact structure of the front section remains uncertain. * Total length: 112 meters (third largest in Fukushima, fourth in Tōhoku). * Outer surface covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|Fukiishi]], though no haniwa have been found. * Burial facility remains unexcavated; excavated artifacts include shards of double-lipped earthenware jars. The tomb's construction is associated with the early expansion of large-scale kofun in southern Tōhoku. Comparisons have been drawn with [[:en:Ōtsukayama Kofun|Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (114 meters) and [[:en:Tōmizuka Kofun|Tōmizuka Kofun]] (110 meters), indicating links to Yamato rulers and regional governance. == Historical Significance == The site has been historically documented in records related to Konkoji Temple and has been surveyed multiple times: * Edo period: Mentioned in temple records of Konkoji. * 1940 (Shōwa 15): First introduced in academic literature. * 1979 (Shōwa 54): Surveyed for size and structure. * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Designated a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. * 2004–2007: Excavations confirmed mound structure and burial context. == Mound Dimensions == According to the 2004–2007 excavation survey: * Total length: approximately 112 meters. * Circular rear section: ** Diameter: approximately 60 meters ** Height: approximately 10 meters * Rectangular front section: ** Length: approximately 54 meters ** Height: approximately 6 meters Previous estimates of 118 meters were revised after precise measurements of the foundation stones. Further investigations may reveal additional extensions. A structure near the mound base has been identified as a possible mourning site for funerary rituals. == Cultural Properties == === Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site === * Tamayama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 1980. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Iwaki City Board of Education (2009). ''Tamayama Kofun: Excavation Report.'' * Kawazaki, Juntoku (1989). ''Japanese Kofun Dictionary.'' Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing. * Heibonsha (1993). ''History and Topography of Japan: Fukushima Prefecture.'' == Related Sites == * {{ill|Tsukamae Kofun|ja|塚前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamae Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki City) - A later kofun, estimated at 95-120 meters. * {{ill|Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585422}} - Regional rulers potentially associated with Tamayama Kofun. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhol ... 5 KB (646 words) - 05:31, 20 January 2025 List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima) | align="center"|[[Tamayama Kofun]]

[[ja:玉山古墳]] [[ja:玉山古墳]] [[ja:玉山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|37.114994|N|140.943828|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Tamayama Kofun''' (玉山古墳, also known as '''Tamayama No.1 Kofun''' or '''Konkoji Kofun''') is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Tamayama Kofun Group|ja|玉山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been designated as a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. This kofun Ranks as the third largest in Fukushima Prefecture and the fourth largest in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}}. It is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}. == Overview == Tamayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tomb ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a flat terrace along the northern bank of the Niida River in eastern Fukushima. Today, it is located behind the main hall of {{ill|Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja|至宝山金光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with parts of the mound modified for cemetery use. Archaeological excavations by the Iwaki City Board of Education from {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004}} to {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007}} revealed key features of the tumulus: * The mound is built in three tiers in the circular section, while the exact structure of the front section remains uncertain. * Total length: 112 meters (third largest in Fukushima, fourth in Tōhoku). * Outer surface covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi}}, though no haniwa have been found. * Burial facility remains unexcavated; excavated artifacts include shards of double-lipped earthenware jars. The tomb's construction is associated with the early expansion of large-scale kofun in southern Tōhoku. Comparisons have been drawn with {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun|en|Ōtsukayama Kofun|WD=Q11381233|lt=Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun}} (114 meters) and {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|en|Tōmizuka Kofun|WD=Q11642380|lt=Tōmizuka Kofun}} (110 meters), indicating links to Yamato rulers and regional governance. == Historical Significance == The site has been historically documented in records related to Konkoji Temple and has been surveyed multiple times: * Edo period: Mentioned in temple records of Konkoji. * 1940 (Shōwa 15): First introduced in academic literature. * 1979 (Shōwa 54): Surveyed for size and structure. * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Designated a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. * 2004–2007: Excavations confirmed mound structure and burial context. == Mound Dimensions == According to the 2004–2007 excavation survey: * Total length: approximately 112 meters. * Circular rear section: ** Diameter: approximately 60 meters ** Height: approximately 10 meters * Rectangular front section: ** Length: approximately 54 meters ** Height: approximately 6 meters Previous estimates of 118 meters were revised after precise measurements of the foundation stones. Further investigations may reveal additional extensions. A structure near the mound base has been identified as a possible mourning site for funerary rituals. == Cultural Properties == === Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site === * Tamayama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 1980. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Iwaki City Board of Education (2009). ''Tamayama Kofun: Excavation Report.'' * Kawazaki, Juntoku (1989). ''Japanese Kofun Dictionary.'' Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing. * Heibonsha (1993). ''History and Topography of Japan: Fukushima Prefecture.'' == Related Sites == * {{ill|Tsukamae Kofun|ja|塚前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamae Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki City) - A later kofun, estimated at 95-120 meters. * {{ill|Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585422}} - Regional rulers potentially associated with Tamayama Kofun. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhol ... 5 KB (646 words) - 05:31, 20 January 2025 List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima) | align="center"|[[Tamayama Kofun]]

[[ja:玉山古墳]] [[ja:玉山古墳]] [[ja:玉山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|37.114994|N|140.943828|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}}} '''Tamayama Kofun''' (玉山古墳, also known as '''Tamayama No.1 Kofun''' or '''Konkoji Kofun''') is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iwaki, Fukushima|en|Iwaki, Fukushima|WD=Q274384|lt=Iwaki, Fukushima|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}, {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|WD=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}. It is part of the {{ill|Tamayama Kofun Group|ja|玉山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun Group|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and has been designated as a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. This kofun Ranks as the third largest in Fukushima Prefecture and the fourth largest in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}}. It is estimated to have been constructed in the mid-4th century during the early {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. == Overview == Tamayama Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tomb ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}) situated on a flat terrace along the northern bank of the Niida River in eastern Fukushima. Today, it is located behind the main hall of {{ill|Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja|至宝山金光寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shihōzan Konkoji Temple|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with parts of the mound modified for cemetery use. Archaeological excavations by the Iwaki City Board of Education from {{ill|2004|en|2004|WD=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}} to {{ill|2007|en|2007|WD=Q2024|lt=2007|1=2007}} revealed key features of the tumulus: * The mound is built in three tiers in the circular section, while the exact structure of the front section remains uncertain. * Total length: 112 meters (third largest in Fukushima, fourth in Tōhoku). * Outer surface covered with {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|WD=Q11621213|lt=Fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}}, though no haniwa have been found. * Burial facility remains unexcavated; excavated artifacts include shards of double-lipped earthenware jars. The tomb's construction is associated with the early expansion of large-scale kofun in southern Tōhoku. Comparisons have been drawn with {{ill|Ōtsukayama Kofun|en|Ōtsukayama Kofun|WD=Q11381233|lt=Aizu Ōtsukayama Kofun|1=Ōtsukayama Kofun}} (114 meters) and {{ill|Tōmizuka Kofun|en|Tōmizuka Kofun|WD=Q11642380|lt=Tōmizuka Kofun|1=Tōmizuka Kofun}} (110 meters), indicating links to Yamato rulers and regional governance. == Historical Significance == The site has been historically documented in records related to Konkoji Temple and has been surveyed multiple times: * Edo period: Mentioned in temple records of Konkoji. * 1940 (Shōwa 15): First introduced in academic literature. * 1979 (Shōwa 54): Surveyed for size and structure. * 1980 (Shōwa 55): Designated a Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site. * 2004–2007: Excavations confirmed mound structure and burial context. == Mound Dimensions == According to the 2004–2007 excavation survey: * Total length: approximately 112 meters. * Circular rear section: ** Diameter: approximately 60 meters ** Height: approximately 10 meters * Rectangular front section: ** Length: approximately 54 meters ** Height: approximately 6 meters Previous estimates of 118 meters were revised after precise measurements of the foundation stones. Further investigations may reveal additional extensions. A structure near the mound base has been identified as a possible mourning site for funerary rituals. == Cultural Properties == === Fukushima Prefectural Historic Site === * Tamayama Kofun - Designated on March 28, 1980. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * Iwaki City Board of Education (2009). ''Tamayama Kofun: Excavation Report.'' * Kawazaki, Juntoku (1989). ''Japanese Kofun Dictionary.'' Tokyo: Tokyodo Publishing. * Heibonsha (1993). ''History and Topography of Japan: Fukushima Prefecture.'' == Related Sites == * {{ill|Tsukamae Kofun|ja|塚前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukamae Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki City) - A later kofun, estimated at 95-120 meters. * {{ill|Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikawa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|1=Ishikami no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11585422}} - Regional rulers potentially associated with Tamayama Kofun. # {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Iwaki, Fukushima) - 112 meters in length. * {{ill|Tamayama Kofun|ja|玉山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayama Kofun|qq=}} (Yotsukura, Iwaki) - Fukushima Prefecture Historic Site. 112-meter keyhol ... 5 KB (646 words) - 05:31, 20 January 2025 List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukushima) | align="center"|[[Tamayama Kofun]]
